title: Azure AI App & Agent Developer Associate (AI-103)
subtitle: Build AI-Powered Applications and Autonomous Agents on Azure
description: Learn to design, build, and deploy AI-powered applications and autonomous agents using Azure AI Foundry, Azure OpenAI Service, and Semantic Kernel
overview: This advanced course prepares you for the AI-103 certification exam by covering the full lifecycle of building AI applications and autonomous agents on Azure. You will learn to architect AI solutions with Azure AI Foundry, implement effective prompt engineering and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) patterns, orchestrate multi-step agents with function calling and Semantic Kernel, and deploy production-grade AI applications with responsible AI safeguards. The course combines hands-on labs with real-world architectural patterns drawn from enterprise AI deployments.

learningObjectives.title: AI Application Architecture & Azure AI Foundry
learningObjectives.description: Design scalable AI application architectures using Azure AI Foundry, selecting appropriate models and configuring endpoints for production workloads

learningObjectives.title: Prompt Engineering & RAG Implementation
learningObjectives.description: Craft effective system prompts, implement retrieval-augmented generation with Azure AI Search, and optimise context windows for accuracy and cost

learningObjectives.title: Agent Development & Orchestration
learningObjectives.description: Build autonomous agents using Semantic Kernel, implement function calling patterns, and orchestrate multi-step workflows with tool integration

learningObjectives.title: Production Deployment & Responsible AI
learningObjectives.description: Deploy AI applications at scale with content filtering, monitoring, rate limiting, and Microsoft Responsible AI safeguards

modules.title: AI App Architecture & Azure AI Foundry
modules.description: Architect AI applications using Azure AI Foundry and Azure OpenAI Service, selecting models and configuring deployments
topics: Azure AI Foundry project setup and model catalogue navigation
Selecting and deploying foundation models for specific use cases
Azure OpenAI Service configuration: endpoints, API keys, and managed identity
Application architecture patterns for AI-powered solutions
SDK integration with Python and C# client libraries
Managing model deployments, quotas, and regional availability
Cost estimation and token consumption planning
Connecting AI Foundry to external data sources and APIs

modules.title: Prompt Engineering & Retrieval-Augmented Generation
modules.description: Master prompt engineering techniques and implement RAG patterns for grounded, accurate AI responses
topics: System prompt design: persona, constraints, and output formatting
Few-shot and chain-of-thought prompting strategies
RAG architecture: chunking, embedding, indexing, and retrieval
Azure AI Search integration for vector and hybrid search
Context window management and token optimisation
Grounding techniques to reduce hallucination
Structured output generation with JSON mode and function definitions
Evaluating prompt effectiveness with automated testing

modules.title: Agent Development & Orchestration
modules.description: Build autonomous agents with Semantic Kernel, implement function calling, and orchestrate complex multi-step workflows
topics: Semantic Kernel architecture: plugins, planners, and memory
Function calling: defining tools, handling invocations, and chaining
Multi-agent orchestration patterns and handoff strategies
Agent memory management: conversation history and long-term recall
Integrating agents with enterprise APIs and data sources
Error handling and retry logic for agent workflows
Testing and debugging agent behaviour in development
Copilot Studio integration for low-code agent surfaces

modules.title: Production Deployment & Responsible AI
modules.description: Deploy AI applications at production scale with monitoring, content safety, and responsible AI compliance
topics: Production deployment patterns: blue-green, canary, and A/B testing
Content filtering configuration and custom blocklists
Rate limiting, throttling, and quota management
Application monitoring with Azure Monitor and Application Insights
Microsoft Responsible AI principles and implementation checklist
Red-teaming and adversarial testing for AI applications
Data privacy and compliance considerations for AI workloads
Scaling AI applications: load balancing and failover strategies

handsOnLabs.title: Azure AI Foundry Project Setup
handsOnLabs.description: Create an AI Foundry project, deploy a foundation model, and build a basic chat application with the Python SDK

handsOnLabs.title: RAG Pipeline Implementation
handsOnLabs.description: Build a retrieval-augmented generation pipeline with Azure AI Search, document chunking, and vector embeddings

handsOnLabs.title: Autonomous Agent with Semantic Kernel
handsOnLabs.description: Build a multi-tool agent using Semantic Kernel with function calling, memory, and orchestrated task completion

handsOnLabs.title: Production Deployment with Safety Controls
handsOnLabs.description: Deploy an AI application with content filtering, monitoring dashboards, and responsible AI evaluations

prerequisites: Programming experience with Python or C#
Familiarity with Azure AI services and the Azure portal
Understanding of REST APIs and HTTP-based integrations
Basic knowledge of machine learning concepts and large language models
targetAudience: Developers building AI-powered applications on Azure
Copilot Studio developers extending agent capabilities
AI engineers designing autonomous agent solutions
Software architects integrating AI into existing applications

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Microsoft Certified Trainer with deep experience building AI-powered applications and agent solutions on Azure. Has architected enterprise AI deployments using Azure OpenAI Service, Semantic Kernel, and Azure AI Foundry across multiple industries.
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate

certification.name: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI App and Agent Developer Associate

personalStory.story: Building AI agents is where cloud development is heading, and I mean that practically, not as a buzzword. Over the past year I have built agents that handle real tasks -- pulling data from APIs, making decisions based on context, and handing off to other agents when the job requires it. The shift from writing prompts to orchestrating autonomous workflows changes how you think about software architecture entirely. This course is built from those production deployments, not from documentation alone.
